word: H6355 - פַּחַת מֹואָב - Pachath Môwʼâb - pakh'-ath mo-awb' - Pahathmoab

sections.paragraph:
Original: פּחת מואב

Transliteration: pachath mô'âb

Phonetic: pakh'-ath mo-awb'

BDB Definition:

Pahath-moab = "pit of Moab"
  1. ancestor of one or two families of Israel who returned from exile in Babylon
  2. a leader of the people, father of the wall rebuilder Hashub, and one who signed the covenant with Nehemiah

Origin: from H6354 and H4124

TWOT entry: None

Part(s) of speech: Proper Name Masculine
